title: Jan.ai Cheat Sheet
description: A practical guide for engineers using Jan.ai, focusing on API, CLI, and prompt techniques.

commands.description: Sets the randomness of the AI's responses.
commands.scenario: When generating creative writing or brainstorming ideas.

commands.description: Uses the Jan 4 model with streaming output.
commands.scenario: When you need real-time responses in a chat application.

commands.description: Simplifies complex topics for better understanding.
commands.scenario: When explaining technical concepts to non-experts.

commands.description: Limits the response length to control verbosity.
commands.scenario: When integrating with systems with character limits.

commands.description: Generates a balanced analysis of a topic.
commands.scenario: When conducting a SWOT analysis or decision-making process.

commands.description: Sets a timeout for API requests to prevent hanging.
commands.scenario: When ensuring API calls do not exceed a certain duration.

commands.command: "Think step by step. Then verify your answer."
commands.description: Encourages logical reasoning and verification.
commands.scenario: When solving complex problems that require detailed solutions.

commands.description: Reduces repetition in generated text.
commands.scenario: When creating diverse content without repeated phrases.

commands.description: Generates a concise summary of a given text.
commands.scenario: When needing a quick overview of lengthy documents.

commands.description: Sets the logging level to debug for detailed logs.
commands.scenario: When troubleshooting or debugging API integrations.

commands.description: Creates code snippets based on a given description.
commands.scenario: When automating code generation for development tasks.

commands.description: Encourages the model to introduce new topics.
commands.scenario: When exploring new ideas or topics in a conversation.

commands.description: Translates text to a specified language.
commands.scenario: When needing quick translations for multilingual support.

commands.description: Retries failed API requests up to three times.
commands.scenario: When ensuring reliability in network communication.

commands.description: Adjusts nucleus sampling to limit the cumulative probability of the next token, reducing the likelihood of nonsensical output.

commands.command: system_prompt="You are a senior software architect. Use concise, technical language."
commands.description: Defines the persona and behavioral constraints at the system level to maintain consistent tone throughout a session.
Offloads specific numbers of model layers to the GPU to optimize inference speed and manage VRAM usage.
Defines custom stop sequences to prevent the model from hallucinating further dialogue turns or unwanted line breaks.

commands.command: Few-Shot Prompting: 'Input: A -> Output: B; Input: C -> Output: D; Input: E -> Output:'
commands.description: Provides a pattern of input-output pairs to guide the model toward a specific format or logic style.
Limits the model's vocabulary choice to the top K most likely next tokens, reducing the probability of 'hallucinated' or nonsensical word choices.
Manually overrides the context window size to prevent memory overflow or to maximize long-document processing capabilities.

commands.command: Chain-of-Verification (CoVe): 'Generate an answer, then identify potential inaccuracies in that answer, then rewrite the answer based on those corrections.'
commands.description: A prompt engineering pattern used to reduce hallucinations by forcing the model to self-audit its own logic.
Explicitly sets the number of CPU threads to use for inference, useful for optimizing performance on multi-core workstations.
A debugging technique to monitor real-time inference errors, model loading issues, or resource allocation failures.
